title: The era of blind faith in big data must end
author: Cathy O'Neil
note: The 'Weapons of Math Destruction' author's TED2017 talk on how secret, important, and harmful algorithms encode bias into lending, hiring, and insurance decisions.

title: Machine Bias
author: Julia Angwin, Jeff Larson, Surya Mattu, Lauren Kirchner (ProPublica)
note: Landmark 2016 investigation of the COMPAS recidivism algorithm, which falsely labeled Black defendants as future criminals at nearly twice the rate of white defendants.

title: Shoshana Zuboff on Surveillance Capitalism (VPRO Documentary)
author: Shoshana Zuboff / VPRO
note: Free 50-minute documentary in which Zuboff explains how tech companies turn behavioral data into prediction products — the chapter's 'data as assets and power' theme.

title: Fairness and Machine Learning: Limitations and Opportunities
author: Solon Barocas, Moritz Hardt, Arvind Narayanan
note: Free MIT Press textbook with video tutorials and course materials covering formal fairness criteria, causality, and US anti-discrimination law for ML systems.

title: Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (GDPR) — Official Text
author: European Parliament and Council
note: The authoritative EUR-Lex text of the GDPR, including all 173 recitals — the primary source for the chapter's discussion of data protection legislation.

title: What is GDPR, the EU's new data protection law?
author: GDPR.eu (Proton AG)
note: Readable overview of the GDPR's seven principles, lawful bases for processing, consent rules, and data subject rights — a gentler entry point than the legal text.

title: Haunted by Data
author: Maciej Ceglowski
note: Strata 2015 talk (cited in DDIA) comparing stored behavioral data to nuclear waste and arguing for data minimization and retention limits.

title: The Moral Character of Cryptographic Work
author: Phillip Rogaway
note: Post-Snowden essay (cited in DDIA) arguing that technical work rearranges power and that engineers bear moral responsibility for enabling or resisting mass surveillance.
